“We Partyin’!”

Kermit Ruffins needs only two words to hype a New Orleans barroom for one of his weekly shows, and this, his personal slogan, sums up what this Big Easy ambassador is all about. From playing himself in the HBO Series Treme, to barbecuing outside his bar, Kermit’s Treme Mother-inLaw Lounge, to sitting in with Jon Batiste and Stay Human on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert, the trumpeter/vocalist keeps himself busy when he isn’t on stage doing what he does best—entertaining and sharing his love of life with the world. He personifies the laid-back vibe of New Orleans.

lespecial is redefining the term “power trio”. The multi-instrumentalists from Connecticut continue to push the boundaries of what a three-piece band is capable of both live and in the studio ahead of their latest album, “Odd Times”. The band’s signature blend of “heavy future groove” combines headbanging metal riffage and surgical rhythmic precision with bone shaking 808s, sub synths and ethereal vocal stylings for a dance floor that welcomes moshing, dancing and hip swaying alike. Listeners are taken on a journey to the musical netherworld through esoteric soundscapes punctuated by raw, primal power.

Odd Times, lespecial’s darkest and heaviest record to date, is an enigmatic and compelling musical project that explores the ever-changing nature of time amidst isolation. With heavy riffs, tribal drumming, and hints of levity, it's a captivating journey crafted in collaboration with Havok's David Sanchez. An intriguing blend of old-school metal and modern djent-inspired riffage awaits in this haunting sonic experience.

MonoNeon, is an American bassist and experimental musician from Memphis, Tennessee. He is known for his presence onYouTube playing bass guitar and being one of the last people to work with Prince. Thomas is a Grammy Award winning artist, for his participation on the 2020 Nas album King’s Disease. 19th Annual Backbeat JazzFest Series presents The Nth Power
Never Alone Album Release Party
categories: Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian
events.description:
Venue: Blue Nile
Starts: 2026-05-04T23:00:00.000-05:00
Categories: Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Classical, Music - Jazz & Blues, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Other

Tickets: https://www.showpass.com/backbeat-jazzfest-series-nth-power/

Monday May 4

Doors 10:00PM / Show 11:00PM

Advance Tickets $20 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.

Day of show $25 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.

The Nth Power believes in the healing power of music. This all-pro alignment of sharp and seasoned musical vets delivers a transcendent blend of soul, funk, rock and rhythm and blues with multi-part vocal harmonies and songs that inspire audiences to dance, groove, make love or just stand there with goose bumps.

The band's electric live performances have been flooring audiences ever since the group formed in New Orleans during an impromptu jam during Jazzfest nearly a decade ago. After an EP, studio album and two live albums, the band returned in 2021 in support of their second full-length studio release, Reverence, which was featured on the first-round nominations ballot for the 2022 Grammy Awards.




The Nth Power hails from diverse musical backgrounds, races and creeds, but the universal language of love and its power to change lives is the thread that binds them. The trio features drummer Nikki Glaspie, whose credentials include Beyonce, Maceo Parker, Dumpstaphunk and countless more; guitarist and lead vocalist Nick Cassarino, known best for his previous work with Jennifer Hartswick and Big Daddy Kane; and bassist Nate Edgar of reggae giant John Brown's Body.

SINGLE RELEASE PARTY

for the NEW HIT “SUNDAY BEST”

Hailing from the 7th Ward neighborhood in New Orleans, Julian “Juice” Gosin is an artist, producer, trumpeter, MC, and educator. Best known for being a longtime member of award-winning and globally renowned brass band The Soul Rebels, Julian has worked with and been featured alongside Metallica, Nas, Lenny Kravitz, Wu-Tang Clan, Katy Perry, Maroon 5, and Maceo Parker. He has also recorded music with artists like Big Freedia, Trombone Shorty, G-Eazy, Phony ppl, and Dave East. 

His recent debut album, Be Intentional, launched his solo career. The self-produced and written album has elements of hip hop, pop, RnB, and dance music and features Trombone Shorty, Nicolas Payton, and Nigel Hall. 

All Shows Ages 21+

Irvin Mayfield is a Billboard and two-time Grammy Award- winning artist, composer, and producer. His 30+ record discography and published written works keynote three decades of world-tours, recorded performances, and collaborations with distinguished artists such as Stevie Wonder, Bob Weir, Frank Ocean, Aaron Neville, Wynton Marsalis, and Trombone Shorty.

A humanist born, raised, and presently residing in New Orleans, LA, Mayfield espouses a daily practice of constant reimagination in his community. His upcoming recording and live performance projects engage global thought leadership edging on the frontiers of art, media, technology and beauty.
